title: Prockia lutea
taxonomy: Prockia lutea is classified as a genus and species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It belongs to the order Sapindales and the class Magnoliopsida. Further classification places it in the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. Prockia lutea is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The exact classification is still being studied and verified by botanists for further accuracy.
uses: Prockia lutea, a flowering plant native to Australia, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. Traditionally, the leaves and stems of Prockia lutea were used to treat fever and rheumatism. The plant's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties make it useful for wound care and skin conditions. Prockia lutea is also used in folk medicine to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Prockia lutea's medicinal uses.

Prockia lutea has been traditionally used in some cultures to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
